What's going on everyone? Welcome to the Abandoned Hope sale. This is the week three sale or week two sale depending on you know whatever your front end program is but it's the halfway point sale. So this is where you're going to be meeting with them halfway through to close them on the next thing. The reason we do this halfway through is that usually this is when people's excitement is the highest, their engagement is the highest and they've had enough to taste of the program that they should be able to make a decision about staying. So we want to maximize our selling opportunities and do this halfway point rather than trying to do it at the end when they are emotionally done. So heat point here, you have to make sure that you plan these so that you are doing them at the halfway point. Alright so note number one before you begin these Abandoned Hope sales, there are Abandoned Hope sales examples in the sales training, they're not in this section and that is because I kind of wanted to keep all the sales stuff together. So if you want examples and recordings of that you can go to the sales module. Those are not of us, those are just Jim owners who entered or submitted those recordings to help other Jim owners. Okay second note, make sure that you have their payment info ready and new contracts printed, meaning you should have their initial contract out so that you have their payment info right there so that you can transcribe it onto a new contract and get their signature on the spot. You don't want to delay the sale of big sales mentor of mine, used to say TKAD which is Time Kills All Deals and so for that reason you want to make sure that you can close the deal in that moment. Couple things before we get going, first off you have to come in with the mindset of there are no obstacles. The pitch that we were giving them is cheaper than what they are currently paying for, their spouse already knows that they've made decisions to get healthy so there's no reason that their spouse would be against it because it is the same thing except cheaper than what they've already signed up for and I'm not sure I need to think about it. They have all the information that they would possibly need, they just worked out for the last three weeks, they know what they need to, they know all the, they have all the info they need. Three more weeks of workouts is not going to tell them anything and so you need to explain that to them so that they can confront the decision to be healthy. All right, the alternative that we're going to be presenting is risk of loss for the same money. Basically we're saying listen we're going to give you a win here and I'll get into that in a moment but essentially the choice here is you can either continue at your own risk or we can make this a win automatically and we can commit to being healthy for good. Right, so it's kind of a, it should be a no-brainer from an offer standpoint. Third is that make sure that you have the up-to-date credit card. I'm resaying this because people sometimes still forget so make sure that you have updated credit card info on this property for each of them to sign. Okay, so these are just kind of some benchmark stats, some ideas that you should know going into this close. First off, you should not, you should 100% be approaching this from the standpoint that it is a sale but it isn't assumed close. Okay, so it should feel natural like this is just what everyone does is the next step in the program, boom, and you're explaining that to them. You should not feel hesitant about it, like you should not feel confrontational, it should be very a matter of fact, it should feel relaxed to you because you should know these people, these are your clients at this point, they should have got an exceptional service so there's no reason that you should feel worried about it. All right, they want to stay, they want to get healthy, you provide health, so don't worry about it. The next thing that they need is to continue doing it, which you know and they know, so there's no reason for them not to sign up and at a cheaper rate. Okay, so you should expect 70 to 80% close rate, that is what most gyms do at this sale, which means that the majority of the sales that you're going to have for continuity are going to come in this consultation. All right, so and the reason for that is because it is assumed close and you're just going to go for it. All right, understand why they need to go for the long haul and I mean this seriously, what do they need? Will they actually succeed on their own? Of course they won't, like why wouldn't they succeed on their own? You have to get them, you have to pull this back to the surface, it's kind of like going back in time to the first sale of reminding them why they walked in the door and all the things they struggled with up to that point, what they didn't have, which they do have now and what they should keep wanting to have so they can get to their goal. Why hadn't they made able to do this before this and removing accountability and it all comes crumbling down. So people are like, well, maybe I'll be able to do it on my own. It's like, let's be real, you did this for the last five years quote on your own and it didn't get you anywhere, it got you in this door. So like let's not make the same mistake twice. Another little hook there if you ever need it is the best people in the world have coaches, I don't feel like if you have a coach it means that you're not good, like everyone has a coach, I have a coach for my business, right? Like everyone has a coach, Michael Jordan has a coach, so thinking that you don't need a coach is silly and prideful. Okay, you really do need to come from a place of compassion because genuinely you should feel like if they do not sign up in this instance, you are failing them really because they need your help to make a decision that they do not yet know how to make for themselves and you're teaching them how to prioritize themselves and teaching them what it means to be coached so that they can be successful and then probably seeing what you is very unrealistic because the reality is they're not going to go to anywhere else, they're going to go back to the couch, they're going to go back to the Netflix, they're going to go back to doing everything else they were doing before, all right? And you have history on your side so you can use that. Fourth, help them confront the decisions and examine alternatives, all right? So you really like you should go for the assumed clothes and most of the times you don't even have to get into this but I want you to come from this perspective of understanding that there is no other alternative, right? They're not going to go to plant fitness, no disrespect to plant fitness but if you're they're not going to go to a self-service facility get the same results they are with a coach checking in on them all the time, period, right? So you want to paint that picture for them, you don't want to be a dick but you want to be very clear like let's confront this like what are the alternatives you have if you don't have any, right? Right? You need to stay here this obvious term meaning when you're setting out your communications you're going to want to make it that way because people have some weird psychological thing with mandatory things they always attend them because since we were little kids when I said mandatory we had to do it so it is a mandatory meaning and I just want you to know that the likelihood that you sell these people is going to be directly proportional to the volume of outreach and the quality of the service they receive at this point so make your job easier by doing a good job, right? Like if you've been crushing it and checking in on them and caring for them and your team has been upbeat and acknowledging them by name and they went to an event with your team like of course they're going to stay it's going to be a no-brainer you're not going to have to get into a really confrontational sale, right? So like make your job easier by just doing a good job before this consultation, all right? Cool! So the abandoned hope sale week three doesn't have to be week three really should say halfway point so halfway point the three components of this is that you're not only going to give them what they they're doing right now and you give it for less but you're also on top of that you're going to stack the alternate play so whatever play you didn't use on the front end you're going to use on the back end so it's like if you did the 42-day detox first you're going to use a six-week challenge on the back if they did the six-week challenge first you're going to use the 42-day detox on the back and you're going to position it as the next step, all right? The second phase of the program, all right? And the reason for that is because people don't like to do things incomplete, right? Like if you're doing only phase one like we still got to go through phase two to make sure that this sticks and so that we can set you up for long-term success let's be real six weeks it's not going to get you to your goal we both know that, right? So like if you've been in it now you actually risk losing gaining back even more weight which is not what we want, right? In this instance you're going to give them an automatic win which is like listen if you understand this is for the life on we want to reward that behavior because you're an action taker, right? And so we're actually going to give you all of the money that you've paid for this program as credit towards staying, all right? So you've got this entire period of time for free it's on us because you committed to staying and actually getting healthy which is what we want and what our facility is all about. We're about to give it to you for free, all right? Boom and then on top of that we're going to give you a lifetime lower rate so we're going to take that so it's like you have a $600 program I'm going to give you that for free we're going to take your your winnings because I'm going to give you this as an automatic win and then we're going to take that winnings and we're going to credit towards a lifetime lower rate so you're going to get a lower rate you're going to get a free that a play that normally costs $600 which is the second phase of this program I'm going to give it to you for nothing and you don't want to make sure you do that because otherwise you have to sell against the fear of getting back, all right? So here's the sales structure like the actual conversation flow. First thing as soon as they come in you've got to be upbeat as hell. They're like, how are you feeling? How's it going? Excited? Like, you know, how's everything? They're going to tell you what's going on no matter what they hit you with you need to overcome like over the top with absolute positive energy like someone comes in down tone you need to be so upbeat that they they can't help but match your energy, okay? So you're going to applaud them any progress at all they're like I've lost two pounds in three weeks and you're like that's amazing how much weight did you lost up at this point?
